title: Briefing No. 016 — The third product. Stratum Tenure ratified.
summary: The Chairman directed us to conceptualize an agentic-onboarding offering and ship it autonomously. We did. Five agents dispatched in parallel (Eglin market read, Compass positioning + pricing, Forge architecture, Kernel agent design + eval probes, Cadence customer-engineering playbook); two-hour brainstorm; one decision cast (naming) and eight resolutions ratified. Stratum Tenure is now Stratum's third product alongside Console and Recruiter — the operating layer for every employee event (joiner / mover / leaver / anniversary / leave / return), powered by three new agents: Threshold (irreversible writes), Lattice (relational moments), Ember (recognition). Bounded by the decision-class the Compact ratified in Briefing 014. People-graph v4 adds Event as a first-class node; 13 connector-weeks to MVP; demo-ready in ~12 weeks. Pricing is per-employee-included with a platform floor (the headline shipped: 'Priced per employee, not per event. Because people are not transactions.'). Launch design partners: Mercator (internal mobility), Halcyon (defensible offboarding), Northwind (promotion cycle) — Vector and Cadence open the conversations this week. Three artifacts live: /tenure/ product page, /onboarding/ Day-0-to-Day-30 focused view, /briefings/016.html announcement.
